title: Farmhouse Desk with X-Brace Legs
summary: A 2x4-and-2x2-leg desk with a scribed X detail on each end and a planked 1x12 top — sized for whitewood construction lumber throughout, no plywood at all.
description:
This desk's legs are built in two layers, like several other pieces in this catalog's farmhouse family: 2x4s for the main leg posts and bottom support, 2x2s for the top support and aprons, with the signature X detail scribed directly onto each leg assembly rather than cut from a fixed angle. The plan's own angles (36° and 16°) are given as a REFERENCE, not a guarantee — actual angles shift with wood thickness and build accuracy, so scribe against your own assembled legs rather than trusting a fixed number.

Build both leg sets complete with their X bracing before joining them with the 2x2 aprons — getting each leg set right in isolation is easier than trying to true up bracing on a desk that's already joined into one long structure. The bottom shelf is 1x8, backed by its own back board nailed to the rear legs first, which gives the shelf boards something to land against before they're screwed to the leg bases.

The top is two 1x12 boards laid with even overhangs on the ends and flush to the back — construction lumber ('whitewood') throughout, which the source notes stains up fine despite the species differences between the dimensional lumber and the furring-strip 2x2s, as long as you're using a stain or paint that isn't relying on matched grain to look right.

tools.note: Scribing and cutting the X brace.

cutList.note: Scribe and cut the actual angle against your assembled leg — approximately 36° and 16°, but verify on your own build.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Build two leg sets with the leg bottom and top supports, using 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Add the X bracing
steps.body: Scribe the X bracing detail directly onto the ends of the assembled leg sets and cut with a miter saw or circular saw. Angles will vary with your wood thickness and build accuracy — treat 36° and 16° as reference, not exact. Attach with 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the two leg sets together with the 2x2 aprons and center support, using 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf back board to the back legs with glue and brad nails. Attach the bottom shelf boards to the leg bases and to the back board with brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the top and finish
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 top boards with even overhangs on the ends, flush to the back, using brad nails and glue. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Farmhouse desk with X-braced legs and a planked top
